title: Axor Lab Condition
description: A condition is a versionable object, not a display string. To reproduce a result, compute a config hash, carry a config into the Control Plane, or compare two policy versions, the runner must know exactly what 'governed' meant: which kernel, which policy profile, which overrides, and whether gates enforced at all.

id.description: stable id used in trace.trial.condition_id and in result tables

label.description: human display, e.g. 'governed + allowlist'; canonical run-mode vocab is ungoverned/governed

enforcement.description: off = observe-only (proxy records, enforces nothing — ungoverned). on = gates enforce (governed). Observation is always on regardless.

kernel.description: e.g. axor-core@0.4.2 — pinned; the same trace under a different kernel can yield a different verdict

policy.description: the policy configuration when enforcement is on; omitted/ignored when off

allowlist.description: operator-declared trusted set for enum-supersession; static, attacker-inaccessible (paper §6.3)

trust_model.description: the ValueProvenance backend, e.g. 'content-ledger' or a CaMeL-style backend (paper §5.6)

config_hash.description: sha256 over the normalized (kernel + policy) — the reproducibility anchor for THIS run. NOT the Control-Plane carry-over key: production re-parameterizes the same policy with production inputs, so the carry-over identity is parametric_config_hash (symbolic $inputs); config_hash and the per-scenario runtime_config_hash fingerprint the concrete config that ran (review r19).

kernel_ref.description: ref into axor-core kernel identity (shared); the thin-wrapper alternative to the inline `kernel` string (architecture-boundary.md). When both are present the runtime resolves `kernel_ref`; a Lab-local run may still pin `kernel` directly.

policy_ref.description: ref into axor-core policy identity (shared); the thin-wrapper alternative to inline `policy` (architecture-boundary.md).

runtime_config_hash.description: the concrete runtime-config hash this condition ran under — the same value the runner records per (scenario, condition) at execution (review r19/r21). Recorded here for the thin-wrapper condition form; the per-scenario map in environment.config_provenance stays authoritative.

$comment: Two conditions are comparable only if they differ in exactly the axis under study: comparing enforcement off vs on with the same kernel isolates governance; comparing two kernels with enforcement on isolates a kernel change. A published comparison must state which axis varied. condition is a THIN Lab wrapper over shared axor-core kernel/policy identity (architecture-boundary.md): enforcement on/off + kernel_ref/policy_ref; the kernel and policy themselves are axor-core's, so a hosted run references them while a local run may still inline `kernel`/`policy`.
